questions.q: Who directed the 1987 Tamil crime film Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Nayakan (1987) was directed by Mani Ratnam.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role of the don in the Tamil film Nayakan (1987)?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the central character, Velu Naicker, in Nayakan.

questions.q: In which year was the Tamil film Nayakan released?
questions.explanation: Nayakan was released in 1987.

questions.q: In which language was the Mani Ratnam film Nayakan originally made?
questions.explanation: Nayakan is a Tamil-language film.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1987 Tamil film Nayakan?
questions.explanation: The music for Nayakan was composed by Ilaiyaraaja.

questions.q: The Tamil film Nayakan is set in which Indian city's underworld?
questions.explanation: Nayakan is set in the underworld of Bombay (Mumbai).

questions.q: What is the name of the main character played by Kamal Haasan in Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan's character in Nayakan is named Velu Naicker (Sakthivel 'Velu' Naicker).

questions.q: Which classic Hollywood film is Mani Ratnam's Nayakan widely said to echo?
questions.explanation: Nayakan is widely noted for echoing Francis Ford Coppola's The Godfather.

questions.q: What genre best describes the 1987 Tamil film Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Nayakan is a crime drama centred on a Bombay underworld don.

questions.q: Real-life Bombay smuggler Varadarajan Mudaliar is often cited as a loose inspiration for the protagonist of which 1987 Tamil film?
questions.explanation: Velu Naicker in Nayakan was loosely inspired by the Bombay don Varadarajan Mudaliar.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for the 1987 Tamil film Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Nayakan was shot by cinematographer P. C. Sreeram.

questions.q: How many National Film Awards did the 1987 Tamil film Nayakan win?
questions.explanation: Nayakan won three National Film Awards: Best Actor, Best Cinematography and Best Art Direction.

questions.q: In 2005, which magazine included Nayakan on its list of the 100 best films of all time?
questions.explanation: Time magazine included Nayakan in its 2005 'All-Time 100 Best Films' list.

questions.q: Which actor played Selvam, Velu Naicker's loyal friend and right-hand man, in Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Janagaraj played Selvam, Velu Naicker's loyal friend and aide, in Nayakan.

questions.q: The 1987 Tamil film Nayakan was India's official entry to the Academy Awards in which category?
questions.explanation: Nayakan was India's official entry for the Best Foreign Language Film at the 60th Academy Awards.

questions.q: Kamal Haasan won a National Film Award for Best Actor for his performance in which 1987 Mani Ratnam film?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan received the National Film Award for Best Actor for Nayakan.

questions.q: Which haunting melody from Nayakan remains among Ilaiyaraaja's most celebrated compositions?
questions.explanation: "Thenpandi Cheemayile" is one of the best-known songs from Nayakan's Ilaiyaraaja score.

questions.q: Nayakan was remade in Hindi in 1988 by Feroz Khan under which title?
questions.explanation: Feroz Khan's 1988 Hindi remake of Nayakan, starring Vinod Khanna, was titled Dayavan.

questions.q: Which actor played the Assistant Commissioner of Police who unknowingly becomes Velu Naicker's son-in-law in Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Nassar played the upright Assistant Commissioner who marries Velu Naicker's daughter in Nayakan, in his breakthrough role.

questions.q: Which actress played Neela, the wife of Velu Naicker, in the 1987 film Nayakan?
questions.explanation: Saranya Ponvannan played Neela, Velu Naicker's wife, in Nayakan, marking her feature film debut.

questions.q: Nayakan opens with Velu Naicker as a boy who flees to Bombay after which event in his life?
options: The killing of his father by police
questions.explanation: Young Velu flees to Bombay after police kill his union-leader father, shaping his hatred of authority.

questions.q: Nayakan was the first major feature-film collaboration between Mani Ratnam and which lead actor?
questions.explanation: Nayakan marked the first film pairing of director Mani Ratnam and actor Kamal Haasan.

questions.q: Who wrote the dialogues for the 1987 Tamil film Nayakan, with Mani Ratnam writing the screenplay?
questions.explanation: Nayakan's screenplay was by Mani Ratnam, with dialogues by writer Sujatha (S. Rangarajan).

questions.q: Besides Best Actor, in which craft category did Nayakan win a National Film Award?
questions.explanation: P. C. Sreeram won the National Film Award for Best Cinematography for Nayakan.

questions.q: Which often-quoted line, asking whether one is good or bad, is spoken to Velu Naicker in Nayakan?
questions.explanation: The famous line "Neenga nallavara, kettavara?" ("Are you a good man or a bad man?") is asked of Velu Naicker.

questions.q: Nayakan's National Film Award for Best Art Direction went to which designer, who recreated the Dharavi slums for the film?
questions.explanation: Art director Thota Tharani won the National Film Award for Best Art Direction for recreating Dharavi in Nayakan.

questions.q: In Nayakan, who is the kind-hearted Dharavi smuggler that takes in and raises the orphaned young Velu after he flees to Bombay?
questions.explanation: Hussain, a generous small-time smuggler in Dharavi, shelters and raises the young Velu after he arrives in Bombay.

questions.q: On which festival day was Nayakan released in 1987?
questions.explanation: Nayakan was released on 21 October 1987, on Diwali day.

questions.q: Nayakan's narrative is set largely in which Tamil-populated neighbourhood of Bombay?
questions.explanation: Much of Nayakan unfolds in Bombay's Dharavi, home to a large Tamil migrant community.

questions.q: Nayakan is frequently ranked among the greatest Indian films and is regarded as a landmark of which industry's crime cinema?
questions.explanation: Nayakan is regarded as a landmark crime film of Tamil cinema.
